title.rendered: Discovering the Treasures of Hudson Valley, New York – feat. Columbia County

content.rendered:
Published by Conde Nast Traveler
www.cntraveler.com/contributor-content/story/disc…

Traveling to New York State isn’t simply about visiting New York City (NYC), but rather about exploring the plethora of scenic places to explore, dine, and unwind. For some, this could involve visiting local art museums or historical sights, while for others, it could be discovering the food and small-town character of Columbia County, New York.

No matter your reason for travel, the Hudson Valley has a rich cultural history, with scenery, architecture, and even world-class art. The best part is that getting there is easier than you might have thought, as Amtrak’s Empire Service stops in the city of Hudson, making the journey from Penn Station a comfortable ride without the need to drive.

Exploring the sights: Where history and landscapes collide

The rich geography of Columbia County lends itself to memorable, place-specific experiences. Olana State Historic Site sits atop 250 acres of rolling hills and forested landscape. Visitors can explore five miles of carriage trails designed by American landscape painter Frederic Church, which lead to sweeping views and the site’s Persian-inspired home.

Beyond Olana, Columbia County offers a broader sense of history and terrain that extends well beyond a single destination. The village of Kinderhook, for example, provides a quieter, historically rooted experience, with the nearby Martin Van Buren National Historic Site giving insight into early American life and leadership. Throughout the county, smaller towns, farmland, and open spaces define the experience—it’s less about density, more about discovery.

While the views alone are enough to justify a visit, those drawn to art and culture will find galleries, local institutions, and community-driven creative spaces woven throughout the region. The area also lends itself to slower moments: picnics, walking trails, and scenic drives that reflect the character of the Hudson Valley.

When your NYC excursion concludes, Columbia County affords visitors a distinctly different pace. Rather than resorts or large-scale attractions, the area is defined by its villages, farmland, and scenic beauty. Outdoor experiences here are quieter and more dispersed, from walking trails and scenic overlooks to seasonal activities and nearby ski areas like Catamount, which operates as a ski slope and summer adventure park.

Enjoying the food: Where flavors are rooted in location

For those who are looking to eat when they visit Columbia County, wineries, distilleries and breweries include relaxed daytime experiences, while evenings can be spent at locally owned restaurants that reflect the region’s agricultural roots.

The area has a broad range of dining options, from farm-driven menus to classic, unfussy meals. Rather than a concentrated dining scene, Columbia County’s food culture is spread across its towns, villages, and the city of Hudson, encouraging visitors to explore as they go.

Unwinding at night: The rooms worth staying for

Lodging in Columbia County reflects the character of the region itself—independent, understated, and rooted in place. Visitors will find inns, guesthouses, and small accommodations spread across villages like Kinderhook and throughout the countryside, rather than large resorts or centralized hotel districts. The landscape is shaped by the Taconic Mountains and the Catskills, with open views, quiet roads, and rural settings that prioritize rest over activity. Evenings here are less about nightlife and more about slowing down, whether that means staying in a historic home, a countryside inn, or a locally run property that reflects the area’s pace and identity.

Whether you’re traveling for business or leisure, Columbia County offers a different perspective, one defined by space, history, and a quieter rhythm. Rather than bustling cityscapes, the experience here is intentionally low-key, shaped by small towns, open landscapes, and local culture.

Create your next visit to Columbia County for an experience you won’t forget!

content.rendered:
As we head into another holiday season, there’s no better time to turn your gaze inward—to the rolling hills, quaint villages, and hidden gems of our own Columbia County. Why brave the crowds in the malls and outlet centers when you can uncover one-of-a-kind gifts right here at home? Our local boutiques, makers, and artisans are brimming with handmade treasures: think hand-forged jewelry, artisanal soaps, custom pottery, and cozy textiles that tell the story of our community. Supporting these creators isn’t just shopping—it’s sustaining the heartbeat of Columbia County.

Hudson

The holidays begin in Hudson with the Basilica Farm & Flea Holiday Market (November 28-30). Launched as an “intentional antidote to the commercial frenzy of Black Friday and big-box retail,” it spotlights local creativity, sustainability, and Hudson Valley artisanal heritage. With more than 120 vendors, you might wrap up all your holiday shopping in one vibrant weekend.

Still more gifts to buy? Experience Hudson’s cherished Winter Walk (December 6). This annual celebration features street performers, food vendors, live music, and a spectacular fireworks finale. Shops and galleries extend special evening hours for holiday browsing. Check out Visit Hudson NY for full details and browse their directory of shopping destinations. 

While Hudson dazzles with its energy, venture into the county’s charming villages and towns for a more laid-back, relaxing shopping escape.

Kinderhook / Valatie

The Historic Village of Kinderhook welcomes the holiday season with Candlelight Night (December 5). Luminaries illuminate the streets, Santa and Mrs. Claus make appearances, and the evening brims with live music, caroling, hayrides, pony rides, a tree-lighting ceremony, tastings of local food and drink, and craft shopping amid decorated historic homes. Book lovers? Head to Kinderhook Books for thoughtfully curated new titles and rare bookish antiques. OK Pantry offers another option for unique gifts.

Visit the home of the Nation’s First Santa Claus Club where the holidays come alive with the Valatie Winterwalk (December 13) for a parade, local vendors, carolers, live music, festive displays, and Santa meet-and-greets. Swing by Blue Moon Chocolate and Gifts or Cross-Eyed Owl Gifts for stocking stuffers, decadent sweets, and one-of-a-kind finds for everyone on your list.

Chatham / New Lebanon

Chatham’s Winterfest (December 13) includes a free screening of The Polar Express at the Crandell Theatre, live music, caroling, Santa visits, horse-drawn wagon rides, ice skating, hot cocoa, treats, and special promotions at shops and restaurants. Venture further north for Christmas trees, wreaths, or décor—plus handcrafted gifts, local foods, and custom charcuterie boards— with a visit at Woodlife Farm Market in New Lebanon.

Hillsdale / Copake / Ancram

The Ancram Winterfest (December 6) features horse-drawn wagon rides, a glowing bonfire with live caroling, and a bustling holiday market with local artisans and vendors. One week later, the Copake Grange Holiday Open House (December 13) transforms the historic hall with twinkling lights, holiday treats, handmade wreaths and gifts, and live music with sing-alongs. Round out your adventure with a stop at the Hillsdale General Store or grab a bite at the Roe Jan Brewing Co.

Claverack / Germantown

Time your visit for Churchtown Dairy’s Holiday Market (December 7). Savor horse-drawn wagon rides, warm drinks, holiday treats, and farm-fresh cheer in a sustainable setting, with local artisans offering gift ideas galore. Join the Winter Solstice Celebration (December 20) at the Olana State Historic Site and be sure to browse the gift shop’s curated selections inspired by the site’s art, history, and the Hudson Valley landscape.

Columbia County’s Craft Beverage

Pressed for time or stumped on the perfect present? Opt for a gift from one of Columbia County’s acclaimed craft beverage producers. Whether your recipient savors handcrafted brews, small-batch Hudson Valley wines, crisp ciders, or artisanal distilled spirits, there’s an option to suit every taste, palate, and budget.

This holiday season, let Columbia County’s markets, events, and independent creators turn your gift-giving into a joyful local adventure. By shopping small, you’re not just checking off your list—you’re fueling the artisans, farms, and families who make our county shine. Embrace the magic close to home, create lasting memories, and give gifts that carry the authentic spirit of the Hudson Valley. Happy holidays from Columbia County Tourism—here’s to a season filled with warmth, wonder, and community!

title.rendered: Tee Off in Columbia County: Discovering the Greens Beyond the Expected

content.rendered:
Columbia County is a hidden gem for golf enthusiasts, boasting a diverse array of courses that cater to every skill level and preference. Several exceptional venues are waiting to be explored, each offering unique challenges, beautiful scenery, and a welcoming atmosphere.

First on the list is Undermountain Golf Course in Copake. This charming 9-hole course is celebrated for its stunning views of the Berkshire Mountains and well-maintained fairways. It’s a fantastic spot for a relaxed round, offering a challenging yet enjoyable experience for both beginners and seasoned golfers amidst breathtaking natural beauty.

Further south, you’ll find the picturesque Copake Country Club. Known for its classic design and friendly ambiance, this course provides an enjoyable golfing experience. Its rolling terrain and strategic bunkering make each hole an engaging challenge, ensuring a memorable day on the links for all who visit.

Nestled in the heart of the county, Meadowgreens Golf Course offers a more laid-back, family-friendly atmosphere. This well-kept 9-hole course is perfect for a quick game, practicing your short game, or introducing new players to the sport. Its accessible layout and welcoming vibe make it a local favorite for a casual round.

For those seeking a comprehensive and challenging experience, Winding Brook Country Club in Valatie stands out. This 18-hole championship course features manicured greens, undulating fairways, and challenging water hazards that demand precision and strategic play. It offers a truly rewarding golf outing in a scenic setting.

Beyond the traditional courses, the NY Golf Park in Livingston offers a unique opportunity for practice and fun. This facility is perfect for honing your swing, enjoying a driving range session, or just having a leisurely time without committing to a full round. It’s an excellent resource for golfers looking to improve their game or simply enjoy some time on the range.

And what about those days when the weather isn’t cooperating? When it’s raining or winter, don’t let the elements stop your game! Check out Hudson Valley Indoor Golf. This state-of-the-art indoor facility allows you to play virtual rounds on world-famous courses, refine your swing with advanced simulators, and keep your skills sharp no matter the season.

Finally, for those looking for a different kind of “golf,” Columbia County also offers exciting disc golf opportunities. Test your throwing skills at Stonykill Disc Golf Course in Chatham, known for its challenging layout and beautiful woodland setting. Another fantastic option is the course located within Lake Taghkanic State Park, offering a scenic and enjoyable round amidst the park’s natural splendor.

Whether you prefer traditional greens, dedicated practice facilities, indoor simulations, or a spirited game of disc golf, Columbia County truly has something for every golf enthusiast. Get out and explore these incredible local venues!

title.rendered: Seven New Restaurants in Columbia County

content.rendered:
Columbia County’s dining scene is thriving, with a diverse array of new restaurants opening over the last several months. From refined wine bars and innovative fusion cuisine to bold brasseries, these seven newcomers reflect the region’s growing culinary creativity. Whether you’re exploring for the day or live nearby, check out these exciting new restaurants across Columbia County, NY.

Banque

Located in the heart of Hudson, NY, Banque offers an opulent dining experience that seamlessly blends European elegance with local charm in a meticulously restored 1927 bank building. The menu showcases seasonally inspired dishes. Complementing the culinary offerings is an artisanal chocolatier program, producing handcrafted chocolates that fuse European techniques with the finest local ingredients. Whether for a refined brunch, an elegant dinner, or a leisurely tea service, Banque provides a luxurious yet welcoming setting for discerning guests seeking an exceptional dining experience. https://banquenewyork.com/

Hamrah’s

Hamrah’s Lebanese restaurant in Kinderhook, NY, offers a genuine taste of Lebanese home cooking. This cozy spot focuses on fresh, flavorful dishes made with quality ingredients, often sourced locally. Expect a menu featuring classic staples like flavorful pita wraps filled with falafel or kafta, satisfying rice bowls, and refreshing salads packed with herbs and vegetables. Their savory hand pies, filled with spinach, meat, or other delights, are a particular highlight. While primarily a lunch and takeaway spot, Hamrah’s also offers prepared foods by the pound, allowing you to bring the delicious flavors of Lebanon home. As a welcomed addition to Kinderhook, Hamrah’s offers a lighter, brighter alternative to heavier fare. https://www.hamrahs.com/

Isola Wine & Tapas

Isola in Kinderhook, NY, presents a delightful escape with its focus on Spanish-inspired small plates and a thoughtfully curated wine list. The atmosphere is warm and inviting, making it an ideal spot for sharing dishes and enjoying conversation. Expect a vibrant menu featuring classic tapas like patatas bravas with their signature aioli, flavorful jamón ibérico, and perhaps some creative, seasonal additions. The wine selection leans towards Spanish and European varietals, perfectly complementing the tapas offerings. Isola provides a taste of Spain in the heart of Kinderhook, perfect for a relaxed evening of good food, good wine, and good company. It’s a popular addition, offering a different culinary flavor to the local scene. https://isolawinebar.com/

Restaurant Manor Rock

A unique dining experience in Hudson, NY, rooted in its own farm in Taghkanic. Restaurant Manor Rock is set within a historic townhouse, the restaurant emphasizes refined dishes where the quality of ingredients truly shines. The ever-changing menu reflects the seasonal bounty of Manor Rock Farm, promising a fresh and inventive culinary journey with each visit. This dedication to sustainable, farm-to-table dining creates a strong connection to the local landscape. While the ambiance is described as quietly luxurious, the focus remains on the pure flavors of the meticulously sourced ingredients, making Restaurant Manor Rock a noteworthy destination for those seeking an authentic and thoughtfully prepared meal in Hudson. https://manorrockfarm.com/restaurant

Parker House 1893

​The Parker House 1893, in Craryville, NY, offers a refined dining experience that seamlessly blends historic charm with contemporary culinary artistry. Housed in a meticulously restored 1865 Victorian residence, the restaurant provides an intimate setting perfect for special occasions or a sophisticated evening out. Guests are treated to a menu that emphasizes seasonal, locally sourced ingredients, ensuring each dish is both fresh and flavorful. The ambiance is enhanced by elegant décor, attentive service, and a commitment to excellence that caters to the discerning palate. Whether you’re seeking a memorable meal or a venue for a private event, Parker House 1893, delivers an exceptional dining experience in the heart of the Hudson Valley. https://www.facebook.com/parker.house1893

Saint Florian

Saint Florian offers a delightful taste of Europe in Hudson, NY, blending bistro charm with a curated wine bar experience. The stylish interior hints at the city’s industrial past, creating a unique ambiance for enjoying their ever-changing menu. You might find yourself savoring perfectly cooked steak frites one evening or indulging in creamy raclette another. Beyond the appealing food and thoughtful wine selection, Saint Florian fosters a warm neighborhood feel, hosting live jazz and winemaker events that make it a true community hub and a wonderful addition to Hudson’s dynamic dining scene. https://saint-florian.com/

Taiga

Taiga will envelope you in a captivating and unique culinary journey, deeply rooted in the “wild forest” with a distinct Russian and Siberian influence. Located in Hudson, NY, Taiga masterfully blends ancient indigenous recipes with European techniques, emphasizing natural and organic ingredients, often sourced locally. With an evolving menu showcasing the bold flavors of the north, guests can expect to dine on game, premium meats, and perhaps foraged specialties. The fare evokes the vast landscapes of Siberia and the heartiness of Russian cuisine. https://taigarestaurant.com/

From bold international flavors to elevated local fare, Columbia County’s newest restaurants showcase the creativity and passion of the region’s culinary talent. Whether you’re looking for cozy or contemporary, there’s something fresh to discover each time you visit.

Elevate your senses and make your reservations now.

title.rendered: How Upstate New York Became The Coolest Fall Destination

content.rendered:
Just a couple of hours upstate in the Hudson Valley, stylish expats in search of a romantic alternative to urban life are invigorating rural towns from Ghent to Germantown – and, along the way, providing compelling new reasons to visit.

Read article here.

title.rendered: Five Arty Upstate New York Towns To Check Out

content.rendered:
If you’ve hopped around all the galleries the city has to offer, you can head out of the five boroughs and see what the more bucolic upstate towns have to offer in the way of art. From touring a former school that’s now a gallery in Columbia County to a day trip to Peekskill, there are many ways to sneak in some culture during a weekend escape.

Read article here.
